# terrible_community_general_java_cert (Data Source)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this data source against
- `keystore_pass` (String) Keystore password.

### Optional

- `attributes` (String)
- `cert_alias` (String) Imported certificate alias. The alias is used when checking for the presence of a certificate in the keystore.
- `cert_content` (String) Content of the certificate used to create the keystore. Exactly one of `cert_url`, `cert_path`, `cert_content`, or `pkcs12_path` is required to load certificate.
- `cert_path` (String) Local path to load certificate from. Exactly one of `cert_url`, `cert_path`, `cert_content`, or `pkcs12_path` is required to load certificate.
- `cert_port` (Number) Port to connect to URL. This is used to create server URL:PORT.
- `cert_url` (String) Basic URL to fetch SSL certificate from. Exactly one of `cert_url`, `cert_path`, `cert_content`, or `pkcs12_path` is required to load certificate.
- `executable` (String) Path to keytool binary if not used we search in PATH for it.
- `group` (String)
- `keystore_create` (Boolean) Create keystore if it does not exist.
- `keystore_path` (String) Path to keystore.
- `keystore_type` (String) Keystore type (JCEKS, JKS).
- `mode` (String)
- `owner` (String)
- `pkcs12_alias` (String) Alias in the PKCS12 keystore.
- `pkcs12_password` (String) Password for importing from PKCS12 keystore.
- `pkcs12_path` (String) Local path to load PKCS12 keystore from. Unlike `cert_url`, `cert_path` and `cert_content`, the PKCS12 keystore embeds the private key matching the certificate, and is used to import both the certificate and its private key into the java keystore. Exactly one of `cert_url`, `cert_path`, `cert_content`, or `pkcs12_path` is required to load certificate.
- `selevel` (String)
- `serole` (String)
- `setype` (String)
- `seuser` (String)
- `state` (String) Defines action which can be either certificate import or removal. When `state=present`, the certificate is always inserted into the keystore, even if there already exists a cert alias that is different.
- `trust_cacert` (Boolean) Trust imported cert as CAcert.
- `unsafe_writes` (String)

### Read-Only

- `cmd` (String) Executed command to get action done.
